summary: Gruber publishes his annual Apple Report Card grades for 2021, giving high marks to Mac (A) and iPhone (A) driven by the Apple silicon transition and solid iPhone 13 upgrades, while expressing serious concern about Apple's software design quality (D) and developer relations (D). The Mac earned top marks for the M1 iMac and MacBook Pro launches, while iPad and Apple Watch received solid but unspectacular B grades. His harshest criticism targets Safari's UI redesign debacle and the state of Shortcuts for Mac, which he says looks like it was made by people who've never used a Mac. He sees Apple's hardware reliability as excellent but warns that software design decisions are becoming deeply troubling.
key_points: Mac and iPhone earn A grades thanks to Apple silicon MacBook Pros, colorful iMacs, and strong iPhone 13 improvements including better battery life
Apple Software Quality gets a D — not for bugs, but for design decisions like the Safari tabs redesign that should never have made it past the whiteboard
Apple Watch Series 3 still being sold at entry level no longer meets Apple's own good/better/best standard
Developer Relations earns a D as App Store resentment builds and rip-off apps persist
Apple TV hardware pricing is out of step — $150 for an A8-based Apple TV HD is indefensible
money_quotes: Apple's software design is starting to scare the hell out of me.
I find it deeply troubling that these Safari UI redesigns ever made it past the whiteboard stage.
What the hell is going on with Shortcuts for Mac? Functionally it's pretty good but design-wise it looks like it was made by people who have never used a Mac.
The iPhone 13 Mini gets longer battery life than the regular-sized iPhone 12. That says a lot.
By today's standards, Apple Watch Series 3 is less than 'good'.
It's a failing of Apple's marketing that more people don't know just how good Apple TV is.
If you want to go all-in on the Apple experience for home automation, it's very muddled what you should do, where you should start, what you should buy.
Resentment over App Store policies continues to build. Rip-off apps continue to appear in the App Store.
key_insight: Apple's hardware excellence in 2021 was undermined by alarming software design missteps — particularly Safari's tab redesign — that suggest a deeper problem with how design decisions are vetted internally.
